channel.title: Rob Burbea Guided Meditations
channel.description: A companion feed of Rob Burbea guided meditations, guided practice sessions, and retreat instructions from Dharma Seed.
channel.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
channel.itunes:summary: A companion feed of Rob Burbea guided meditations, guided practice sessions, and retreat instructions from Dharma Seed.

item.title: True to Your Deepest Desires (Talk and Short Guided Meditation)
item.description:
Rob Burbea speaks about jhāna practice through the lens of desire, comparison, and self-view, asking what actually supports practice over time. He argues that the most important work may be learning how to relate to longing, frustration, and difficulty without turning them into a verdict about the self. The talk closes with a short guided meditation that turns toward the beauty of desire, patience, and the one who keeps showing up.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the Question 04:54 Culture and Self-View 08:57 You Can Do This 15:13 View of Practice 27:27 Radical Intention 32:13 Desire and Frustration 39:53 Sorting the Signals 46:18 A Life Shaped by Wanting 58:55 Desire Through Dukkha
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60889/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary:
Rob Burbea speaks about jhāna practice through the lens of desire, comparison, and self-view, asking what actually supports practice over time. He argues that the most important work may be learning how to relate to longing, frustration, and difficulty without turning them into a verdict about the self. The talk closes with a short guided meditation that turns toward the beauty of desire, patience, and the one who keeps showing up.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the Question 04:54 Culture and Self-View 08:57 You Can Do This 15:13 View of Practice 27:27 Radical Intention 32:13 Desire and Frustration 39:53 Sorting the Signals 46:18 A Life Shaped by Wanting 58:55 Desire Through Dukkha
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60889/

item.title: Metta Practice, and a few things about Piti (Instructions)
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a practical talk on using metta as a springboard into jhana, while clarifying how that purpose differs from cultivating metta for its own sake. He then turns to piti: what it is, how it can arise in different ways, and how to work with it once it is present through spread, narrow probing, and open receiving. The talk stays close to practice, with repeated emphasis on sensitivity, attunement, enjoyment, and not forcing the process.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the frame 00:57 Metta as springboard 04:56 Attunement and phrases 10:22 From metta to joy 11:51 What piti is 14:19 How piti arises 19:30 Let it pervade 21:04 Two modes of attention 25:07 Practice at the edge
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60871/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary:
Rob Burbea offers a practical talk on using metta as a springboard into jhana, while clarifying how that purpose differs from cultivating metta for its own sake. He then turns to piti: what it is, how it can arise in different ways, and how to work with it once it is present through spread, narrow probing, and open receiving. The talk stays close to practice, with repeated emphasis on sensitivity, attunement, enjoyment, and not forcing the process.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the frame 00:57 Metta as springboard 04:56 Attunement and phrases 10:22 From metta to joy 11:51 What piti is 14:19 How piti arises 19:30 Let it pervade 21:04 Two modes of attention 25:07 Practice at the edge
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60871/

item.title: Breathing with the Energy Body (Guided Meditation)
item.description:
This guided meditation explores the whole body as an energy body, using posture, breath, and imagination as mutually shaping supports for samadhi. Rob Burbea moves slowly through different points in the body—navel, solar plexus, heart, throat, crown, neck, back, and sacrum—while emphasizing sensitivity to tone, vibration, and what feels helpful. The talk closes with a short teaching section on how to hold these methods lightly and keep a clear sense of what is primary and what is secondary in practice.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the posture 02:50 Whole-body awareness 05:38 Long breath and expansion 09:32 Breath at the navel 13:14 Moving through the midline 17:53 Heart and throat tour 23:46 Crown, neck, and back 34:07 Sacrum and release 37:20 Practice reflections
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60862/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary:
This guided meditation explores the whole body as an energy body, using posture, breath, and imagination as mutually shaping supports for samadhi. Rob Burbea moves slowly through different points in the body—navel, solar plexus, heart, throat, crown, neck, back, and sacrum—while emphasizing sensitivity to tone, vibration, and what feels helpful. The talk closes with a short teaching section on how to hold these methods lightly and keep a clear sense of what is primary and what is secondary in practice.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the posture 02:50 Whole-body awareness 05:38 Long breath and expansion 09:32 Breath at the navel 13:14 Moving through the midline 17:53 Heart and throat tour 23:46 Crown, neck, and back 34:07 Sacrum and release 37:20 Practice reflections
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60862/

item.title: The Energy Body and the Whole-body breath (Instructions and Guided Meditation)
item.description:
Rob Burbea begins with a short explanation of why this retreat will focus on whole-body breath and the energy body as supports for samadhi. He then leads a long guided meditation, moving from posture and whole-body awareness into the felt qualities of breath, including expansion, energization, relaxation, and subtle energy currents. In the closing talk, he returns to the same theme in practical terms: experiment rather than default, and let attention become lighter, more responsive, and more subtle both on and off the cushion.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the approach 02:41 Whole body and energy 05:06 A teaching story 12:04 Posture and presence 18:46 Breath in the whole body 27:05 Solar plexus play 34:02 What feels best 40:17 Review and principle 47:59 Daily life practice
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60884/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary:
Rob Burbea begins with a short explanation of why this retreat will focus on whole-body breath and the energy body as supports for samadhi. He then leads a long guided meditation, moving from posture and whole-body awareness into the felt qualities of breath, including expansion, energization, relaxation, and subtle energy currents. In the closing talk, he returns to the same theme in practical terms: experiment rather than default, and let attention become lighter, more responsive, and more subtle both on and off the cushion.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the approach 02:41 Whole body and energy 05:06 A teaching story 12:04 Posture and presence 18:46 Breath in the whole body 27:05 Solar plexus play 34:02 What feels best 40:17 Review and principle 47:59 Daily life practice
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60884/

item.title: Counting Within the Breath (Guided Meditation)
item.description:
Rob Burbea offers a guided meditation built around counting within the breath, moving through a longer count, then a shorter one, while keeping whole-body awareness open. After the practice, he explains why this method can steady attention, counter inertia, and support the conditions for jhana. He also gives practical retreat guidance on posture, movement, interviews, and how to work with comfort and energy.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 01:45 Counting Within Breath 29:27 Settling After Practice 30:31 Why It Helps 31:57 Attention and Jhana 33:48 Posture and Comfort 35:48 Movement and Energy 37:21 Interviews and Practice Focus 40:10 Practical Questions
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60863/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary:
Rob Burbea offers a guided meditation built around counting within the breath, moving through a longer count, then a shorter one, while keeping whole-body awareness open. After the practice, he explains why this method can steady attention, counter inertia, and support the conditions for jhana. He also gives practical retreat guidance on posture, movement, interviews, and how to work with comfort and energy.
Location: Gaia House
Chapters: 01:45 Counting Within Breath 29:27 Settling After Practice 30:31 Why It Helps 31:57 Attention and Jhana 33:48 Posture and Comfort 35:48 Movement and Energy 37:21 Interviews and Practice Focus 40:10 Practical Questions
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/60863/

item.title: Intention and Devotion (Guided Meditation)
item.description:
(Gaia House) Please Note: This series of talks is from a retreat led by Rob Burbea and Catherine McGee for experienced practitioners. The requirements for participation included some understanding of and working familiarity with practices of emptiness, samatha, metta, the emotional/energy body, and the imaginal, as well as basic mindfulness practice. Without this experience it is possible that the material and teachings from this retreat will be difficult to understand and confusing for some.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/43942/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea

Without this experience it is possible that the material and teachings from this retreat will be difficult to understand and confusing for some. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Working with the Emotional Body (Instructions and Guided Meditation: Day Seven)
item.description:
(Gaia House) This series of guided meditations and instructions presents a set of tools and approaches for working skilfully with emotions and mind states in practice.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/13854/

item.title: Guided Meditation: The Vastness of Awareness
item.description:
(Gaia House) Please note that these talks are from a 4 week retreat for experienced meditators. The talks and meditations can be listened to in any order or individually, but as they progressively unfold different levels of understanding of Emptiness, they will probably be more fully understood and the practices more easily developed if taken in series.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/9544/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea

Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Second Morning Instructions and Guided Meditation
item.description:
(Gaia House) As we learn to develop concentration in meditation, samatha (calm, tranquility) is also developed, and together these qualities become a powerful means for deep insight and a source of profound well-being. This progressive series of talks, guided meditations and instructions explores in some detail the art of concentration, primarily through different ways of working with the breath and the body to open to deeper and deeper levels of calmness, presence and joy.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11512/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) As we learn to develop concentration in meditation, samatha (calm, tranquility) is also developed, and together these qualities become a powerful means for deep insight and a source of profound well-being. This progressive series of talks, guided meditations and instructions explores in some detail the art of concentration, primarily through different ways of working with the breath and the body to open to deeper and deeper levels of calmness, presence and joy. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) As we learn to develop concentration in meditation, samatha (calm, tranquility) is also developed, and together these qualities become a powerful means for deep insight and a source of profound well-being. This progressive series of talks, guided meditations and instructions explores in some detail the art of concentration, primarily through different ways of working with the breath and the body to open to deeper and deeper levels of calmness, presence and joy. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Guided Meditation - Directing Love Towards dharmas
item.description:
(Gaia House) This series of talks and guided meditations explores the development of the practices of both Lovingkindness and Compassion, with particular emphasis on the radical possibilities of Awakening that they bring. Through these practices we come to develop deep and beautiful qualities of heart as a real resource both for ourselves and the world, and also open ourselves to the profound and liberating understandings that can emerge from this path of love.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11957/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) This series of talks and guided meditations explores the development of the practices of both Lovingkindness and Compassion, with particular emphasis on the radical possibilities of Awakening that they bring. Through these practices we come to develop deep and beautiful qualities of heart as a real resource both for ourselves and the world, and also open ourselves to the profound and liberating understandings that can emerge from this path of love. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

Through these practices we come to develop deep and beautiful qualities of heart as a real resource both for ourselves and the world, and also open ourselves to the profound and liberating understandings that can emerge from this path of love.
Location: Gaia House

Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/11952/

Through these practices we come to develop deep and beautiful qualities of heart as a real resource both for ourselves and the world, and also open ourselves to the profound and liberating understandings that can emerge from this path of love. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Third Samatha Instructions and Guided Meditation

Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed

item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Metta to phenomena
item.description:
(Gaia House) This series of talks and guided meditations explores the development of the practices of both Lovingkindness and Compassion, with particular emphasis on the radical possibilities of Awakening that they bring. Through these practices we come to develop deep and beautiful qualities of heart as a real resource both for ourselves and the world, and also open ourselves to the profound and liberating understandings that can emerge from this path of love.
Location: Gaia House
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/12292/
item.itunes:author: Rob Burbea
item.itunes:summary: (Gaia House) This series of talks and guided meditations explores the development of the practices of both Lovingkindness and Compassion, with particular emphasis on the radical possibilities of Awakening that they bring. Through these practices we come to develop deep and beautiful qualities of heart as a real resource both for ourselves and the world, and also open ourselves to the profound and liberating understandings that can emerge from this path of love. Location: Gaia House. Source: Dharma Seed.
